compatibleScales.relationship: Primary pentatonic scale built on the root
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains the most consonant notes of A major (A, B, C#, E, F#), avoiding tension notes. Creates an instantly recognizable, bright sound that works universally.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres; foundation of rock, country, blues, and pop. Used extensively by guitarists from B.B. King to John Mayer.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Country, Pop, Folk, Gospel, R&B
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect starting point for improvisation in A. Guaranteed consonance, works over A major chord progressions. Ideal for melodic hooks and solos.

compatibleScales.relationship: Primary minor pentatonic scale built on the root
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains the essential minor pentatonic notes (A, C, D, E, G), providing a dark, bluesy sound. Most common scale for rock and blues improvisation.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, metal, hard rock. The go-to scale for electric guitar solos from Led Zeppelin to Metallica. Essential blues sound.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Metal, Hard Rock, Southern Rock, Grunge, Alternative
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for rock and blues improvisation. Works perfectly over Am, A7, or minor key progressions. Can be used over major keys for bluesy tension.

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major scale of the key
compatibleScales.rationale: The fundamental major scale containing all seven notes (A, B, C#, D, E, F#, G#). Foundation of Western music theory and harmony in the key of A.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres from classical to pop. The default scale for A major harmony and the basis for all diatonic progressions.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Pop, Rock, Folk, Country, Jazz, Musical Theater
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use as the foundation for any A major composition or improvisation. Essential for melodic development and understanding key relationships.

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel minor scale to A major
compatibleScales.rationale: The natural minor scale (A, B, C, D, E, F, G) provides the pure minor sound. Different from A major by three notes (C#→C, F#→F, G#→G).
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, metal, classical, folk music. The foundation of minor key harmony used from Bach to modern rock. Creates melancholic, serious mood.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Metal, Classical, Folk, Alternative, Gothic, Dark Wave
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary choice for A minor compositions. Essential for understanding minor key harmony and for creating dark, emotional melodies.

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor pentatonic with added b5 blue note
compatibleScales.rationale: A minor pentatonic (A, C, D, E, G) plus Eb blue note. This added tritone creates the characteristic blues sound with its distinctive tension and release.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz blues. The signature sound of blues music from Robert Johnson to Stevie Ray Vaughan. Essential for authentic blues phrasing.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Soul, R&B
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for blues improvisation. Use the Eb as a chromatic passing tone between D and E. Perfect over A7 blues progressions.

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor scale with raised 6th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with F→F# (major 6th). Creates a brighter, jazzier minor sound than Aeolian. One of the most versatile minor modes.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, funk, rock, fusion. The go-to minor scale for jazz improvisation. Used extensively in Santana, Miles Davis, and fusion artists.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Funk, Fusion, Latin, Soul, Jazz Rock, Progressive Rock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Primary choice for minor ii chords and Am7 vamps. More sophisticated than natural minor. Perfect for modal improvisation over Am7.

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with lowered 7th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: A major with G#→G (dominant 7th). Creates the characteristic dominant 7th chord sound. Essential for blues, rock, and dominant harmony.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk, country. The signature sound of dominant chords from The Beatles to The Grateful Dead. Essential for I7-IV7 progressions.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Country, Funk, Jam Band, Folk Rock, Americana
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over A7 chords and dominant vamps. Use when you want major sound with bluesy edge. Essential for rock and blues rhythm guitar.

compatibleScales.relationship: Major scale with raised 4th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: A major with D→D# (augmented 4th). Creates a bright, ethereal, dreamy quality. The #4 provides a distinctive uplifting character.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, progressive rock, fusion. Creates dreamlike, floating quality. Common in Joe Satriani, Steve Vai, and film composers like John Williams.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Progressive Rock, Fusion, Film Music, Dream Pop, Neo-Soul
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use over Amaj7 or Amaj7#11 chords. Perfect for I chord modal vamps. Emphasize the D# for characteristic Lydian color.

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with raised 7th degree
compatibleScales.rationale: A natural minor with G→G# (major 7th). Creates strong V7-i resolution. The augmented 2nd interval (F-G#) gives exotic, dramatic character.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Classical, neoclassical metal, flamenco, Middle Eastern. Used extensively in classical music from Bach to modern metal. Dramatic, exotic sound.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Neoclassical Metal, Flamenco, Middle Eastern, Gypsy Jazz, Tango
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for classical-style minor key compositions. Perfect for V7-i cadences. Use when you want traditional minor with strong resolution.

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with raised 6th and 7th degrees
compatibleScales.rationale: A natural minor with F→F# and G→G#. Removes the augmented 2nd of harmonic minor, creating smoother melodic motion. Jazz minor sound.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, fusion, contemporary classical. The foundation of modern jazz improvisation. Essential for bebop and post-bop styles.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Fusion, Contemporary Classical, Jazz Fusion, Smooth Jazz, Modern Jazz
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Essential for jazz minor harmony. Use ascending melodically. Perfect over Am(maj7) chords. Foundation for jazz minor modes.

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural minor with lowered 2nd degree
compatibleScales.rationale: Natural minor with B→Bb (minor 2nd). Creates dark, Spanish, Middle Eastern sound. The b2 provides distinctive exotic character.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, Spanish music, Middle Eastern. Essential for Phrygian dominant sound. Used in heavy metal and Spanish guitar music.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Metal, Spanish, Middle Eastern, Thrash Metal, Progressive Metal
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for exotic, dark minor sounds. Use over Am with Bb emphasis. Essential for flamenco and metal rhythm guitar.

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative minor - vi scale, shares all notes with A major
compatibleScales.rationale: F# minor is the vi of A major. Shares all seven notes but centered on F#. Provides natural minor alternative center in A major key.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres where relative minor relationships apply. Essential for understanding key modulation and minor sounds within major keys.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Pop, Rock, Folk, Singer-Songwriter, Indie, Alternative
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want minor sound while staying in A major key. Perfect for vi-IV-I-V progressions. Natural minor alternative.

compatibleScales.relationship: IV chord scale - subdominant to tonic relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: D major is the IV of A major. Shares 6 of 7 notes (only C#→C). Essential subdominant function in A major harmony.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; fundamental to I-IV-V progressions. Essential in folk, rock, country from The Beatles to Taylor Swift.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Folk, Rock, Country, Pop, Americana, Singer-Songwriter, Classic Rock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for IV chord emphasis. Essential for I-IV-V progressions. Use when moving to/from D major chords.

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale - dominant to tonic relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major is the V of A major. Shares 6 of 7 notes (only G#→G). Creates natural dominant tension resolving to A.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for V-I cadences. Fundamental to Western harmony from classical to contemporary music.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Pop, Rock, Jazz, Country, Folk, Blues
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when moving to/from E major chords. Essential for dominant function and perfect authentic cadences in A.

compatibleScales.relationship: V7 scale - shares all notes with A major
compatibleScales.rationale: E Mixolydian is the V mode of A major (contains D instead of D#). Shares all notes with A major parent scale. Perfect V7 sound.

compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Funk, Country Rock, Jam Band, Southern Rock
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over E7 chords in A major. Essential for dominant-tonic resolution. Use for bluesy V7 sounds.

compatibleScales.relationship: ii chord scale in A major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: B Dorian/Natural minor in context of A major. The ii chord in A provides minor subdominant function. Essential for ii-V-I progressions.

compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Pop, R&B, Neo-Soul, Funk, Contemporary
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over Bm7 chords in A major. Essential for ii-V-I progressions (Bm7-E7-Amaj7). Jazz and contemporary standard.

compatibleScales.relationship: iii chord scale in A major - shares all notes
compatibleScales.rationale: C# Phrygian is the iii mode of A major. Provides exotic minor sound within A major system. Less common but effective for color.

compatibleScales.relationship: 5th mode of D harmonic minor - exotic dominant sound
compatibleScales.rationale: Phrygian with major 3rd (C#). Creates Spanish, Middle Eastern, exotic dominant character. The b2-3 half step is characteristic.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Flamenco, metal, Middle Eastern music. Signature sound of Spanish guitar and heavy metal. Very distinctive exotic flavor.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Flamenco, Metal, Middle Eastern, Spanish, Progressive Metal, Fusion

compatibleScales.relationship: 4th mode of E melodic minor - bright dominant sound

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative minor pentatonic - built on the 5th

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, country. Creates bluesy sound over major chords. Used extensively in rock guitar from Clapton to Mayer.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Country, Blues Rock, Southern Rock, Classic Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains only the most consonant notes of A major (A, B, C#, E, F#), avoiding the 4th (D) and 7th (G#) which create tension. Completely safe for any major harmony.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Universal across all genres; creates bright, optimistic melodies. Especially effective in rock, blues, country, and pop from Chuck Berry to modern pop.

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with raised 4th degree (D becomes D#)
compatibleScales.rationale: Shares all notes except D→D#, creating a brighter, more ethereal major sound. The #4 creates a characteristic dreamy, floating quality.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Jazz, film scores, progressive rock; sounds dreamlike and uplifting. Common in Joe Satriani and Steve Vai guitar solos. John Williams uses it extensively.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Jazz, Film Music, Progressive Rock, Fusion, Dream Pop, Neo-Soul

compatibleScales.relationship: V chord scale - dominant to tonic relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: E major is the V of A major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only G#→G). Creates natural dominant tension that resolves to A major.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; essential for V-I cadences. Fundamental to Western harmony from Bach to contemporary music.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Classical, Pop, Rock, Jazz, Country, Folk, Blues

compatibleScales.relationship: IV chord scale - subdominant to tonic relationship
compatibleScales.rationale: D major is the IV of A major; shares 6 of 7 notes (only C#→C). Provides essential subdominant function in A major harmony.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All genres; fundamental to I-IV-V progressions. Essential sound in folk, rock, and country music.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Folk, Rock, Country, Pop, Americana, Classic Rock, Singer-Songwriter
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for IV chord emphasis in A major. Use when moving to/from D major chords. Foundation of I-IV-V progressions.

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with lowered 7th degree (G# becomes G)
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference (G#→G), creating dominant 7th character. Combines major brightness with bluesy edge.
compatibleScales.musicalContext: Rock, blues, funk; dominant 7th chord vamps. Signature sound of The Beatles, Grateful Dead, and jam bands.
compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Funk, Country, Jam Band, Folk Rock, Americana

compatibleScales.relationship: ii modal scale - shares all notes with A major
compatibleScales.rationale: B Dorian is the ii of A major (second mode). Shares all seven notes. Essential for ii-V-I progressions in A.

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect over Bm7 chords in A major context. Essential for jazz and funk minor chord improvisation.

compatibleScales.rationale: C# Phrygian is the iii of A major (third mode). Provides exotic, Spanish-flavored minor sound within A major system.

compatibleScales.relationship: V modal scale - shares all notes with A major
compatibleScales.rationale: E Mixolydian is the V mode of A major. Perfect dominant 7th sound for A major. Shares all notes with parent scale.

compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Rock, Blues, Funk, Country Rock, Jam Band, Southern Rock

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode to F# Aeolian with raised 6th

compatibleScales.rationale: G# Locrian is the vii° of A major. Provides diminished quality for leading tone chords. Less commonly used but theoretically complete.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Blues, rock, jazz blues. Creates authentic blues sound over major chords. Essential for blues-rock fusion.

compatibleScales.relationship: Built on the V - all notes diatonic to A major
compatibleScales.rationale: E minor pentatonic (E, G, A, B, D) works perfectly over A major. All notes are in A major scale, creating bluesy major sound.

compatibleScales.rationale: Creates exotic major sound with minor 6th. The augmented 2nd interval (E-F#) provides Middle Eastern character.

compatibleScales.relationship: Diatonic subset - scale tones 1, b3, 4, 5, b7 only
compatibleScales.rationale: Contains the core minor pentatonic notes (A, C, D, E, G), all consonant with Dorian. Removes the characteristic major 6th (F#) but remains completely compatible.

compatibleScales.relationship: Parent major scale (A Dorian is the second mode of G major)
compatibleScales.rationale: A Dorian is ii in G major system; shares all notes (A, B, C, D, E, F#, G). Understanding this relationship opens up the entire G major key center.

compatibleScales.relationship: Parallel mode with lowered 6th (F# becomes F)
compatibleScales.rationale: One note difference from Dorian (F#→F). Creates darker, more traditional minor sound. Aeolian is more somber than Dorian.

compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Use when you want darker minor sound than Dorian. Perfect for heavy, dramatic minor music. More traditional than Dorian.

compatibleScales.relationship: A minor pentatonic with added b5 (Eb)
compatibleScales.rationale: Minor pentatonic plus Eb blue note. All notes work over Dorian. The blue note adds characteristic blues tension.

compatibleScales.musicGenreRecommendations: Blues, Rock, Blues Rock, Jazz Blues, Soul, Funk
compatibleScales.recommendedUse: Perfect for blues improvisation over A Dorian. Use Eb as passing tone. Essential blues-rock minor scale.

compatibleScales.relationship: Minor mode with lowered 2nd (B becomes Bb)

compatibleScales.relationship: IV of parent G major - shares all notes
